review:
We stopped by on a whim because we wanted to try a nice restuarant with a great view in Philly. And boy...were we pleasantly surprised! I rarely give 5 stars so this is legit an awesome place.

We didn't make reservations in advance, but we were allowed to sit at the bar or lounge area. It was a surprisingly nice spot because the lounge area had a great view. You get the full dinner menu at the bar/lounge too. 

It comes with a free bread basket with butter. The bread was ok....a bit too hard. We ordered the 20 oz ribeye ($48) made medium rare which was the best steak I've ever had in my life. It was crispy on the outside but the tenderest and juiciest meat on the inside. The lobster mac and cheese ($20) was good with a lot of lobster pieces but it was placed on a narrow boat dish. Which made it difficult to get the mac and cheese off the plate. I would pass on the mac and cheese next time and stick with the ribeye. The warm vanilla cake ($12) was delicious! The cake had a center filling which looked like custard but not as sweet. It had a side of vanilla ice cream. 

This place was phenomenal! Perfect for date night or a special occasion. Next time, I'm going to make reservations for a window seat.
